codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original failed fix (`dc5836d`) and the rerun fix (`cc42740`). The rerun directly closes the missed forward-slash Windows drive-path case by accepting either slash style after the drive letter and by extending the regression inputs to cover `C:/Users/...` and `D:/a/...`. The updated detector still preserves the existing Unix-root and backslash-style Windows coverage, and I did not find any remaining correctness issue within the scoped expected behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared `docs_audit` command still omits the `docs_contract` suite even though the validation contract treats `root_export_parity` and `docs_contract` together as the parity-adjacent docs audit surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-agents-rust/.factory/services.yaml:7-8 defines `docs_audit` as `cargo test -q -p openai-agents --test behavior_parity --test root_export_parity` while `docs_contract` remains a separate command; /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/c8045f72-1cc6-4f08-ad3c-681b35cde0a4/validation-contract.md:170-177 requires `cargo test -q -p openai-agents --test root_export_parity --test docs_contract` for VAL-AUDIT-003 and `cargo test -q -p openai-agents --test docs_contract` for VAL-AUDIT-004.

summary: Pass. The rerun fix addresses the exact blocker from the prior failed review: forward-slash Windows drive absolute paths such as `D:/a/...` are now rejected by the docs portability detector and covered by regression inputs, while the previously added Unix-root and backslash Windows cases remain intact. I also reviewed the fix transcript skeleton and found it consistent with the parity-audit-worker procedure for this scoped follow-up.
